name: Imam Baqir (AS)
title: Baqir al-Ulum
shortDescription: The fifth Imam of the Shia who was present at the event of Karbala at the age of four and witnessed all the tragedies of Ashura with his own eyes.
fullStory:
Muhammad ibn Ali (AS), known as Baqir al-Ulum, is the fifth Imam of the Shia and the son of Imam Sajjad (AS). He was born in 57 AH in Medina and was a child of about four years old during the event of Karbala.

Imam Baqir's (AS) presence in Karbala at such a young age made him a living, eyewitness to Ashura. With his little eyes, he felt with his entire being the severe thirst of the children, the cruelty of the Kufan soldiers, and the oppressed martyrdom of his grandfather Imam Hussain (AS) and his companions.

After Ashura, this young child was taken captive along with the other women and children of the Ahl al-Bayt (AS). Enduring the suffering of the captivity journey from Karbala to Kufa and from Kufa to Sham, on foot and on barebacked camels, left a profound impact on his soul.

In later years, Imam Baqir (AS) always kept the memory of Ashura alive and recounted the tragedies of his grandfather in numerous narrations. By founding the Shia scientific movement, he continued the path of the Master of Martyrs (AS) in the arena of raising awareness and fighting the distortion of religion.

His martyrdom occurred in 114 AH, while up to the last moment of his life, he remained a narrator and messenger of Karbala's oppression.
quoteArabic: قُتِلَ جَدِّيَ الحُسَيْنُ (ع) وَلِيَ أَرْبَعُ سِنينَ...
quote: (From Imam Baqir:) My grandfather Hussain (AS) was killed while I was four years old, and I remember his tragedy.
